title: ranunculus breyninus var. nemorosus
intro: Ranunculus breyninus var. nemorosus, also known as woodland buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus breyninus var. nemorosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
